page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.large is a burstable virtual machine instance based on the KVM hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 2 physical cores, providing 2 vCPUs with 1.0 thread per core, and is paired with 4.0 GB of system memory. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place this instance consistently in the bottom 10% across all evaluated metrics. The Intel Xeon 8163 CPU achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 922.69 and a multi-core score of 1862.68. Memory bandwidth is limited, with uncached large-block read and write speeds of 14.22 GB/sec and 13.09 GB/sec respectively. Geekbench 5 testing yields a single-core score of 721.0 and a multi-core score of 1191.0. For database operations, Redis SET performance is measured at 0.69 million operations per second, while static web serving throughput reaches 0.43 GB/sec.

Due to its burstable CPU allocation and low resource density, the ecs.t5-lc1m2.large is designed as a budget-friendly option for workloads with low baseline CPU utilization that experience occasional spikes. The primary tradeoff is its highly constrained processing and memory throughput, making it unsuitable for sustained high-performance computing, large-scale databases, or complex machine learning tasks. It is best suited for lightweight applications such as basic web serving, development or testing environments, and simple background microservices.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.t5-lc1m2.large is a burstable, KVM-hypervised virtual machine designed for low-intensity workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz, this x86_64 instance features 2 physical cores and 4.0 GB of RAM, with no local storage or GPU resources. Benchmark evaluations place its performance in the bottom 10% tier, with a stress-ng single-core score of 922.69 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 14.22 GB/sec. Given its burstable CPU allocation and baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps, this instance offers a cost-efficient profile for entry-level tasks. It is best suited for lightweight applications, development environments, and low-traffic static web serving.

tagline: A low-cost, burstable virtual machine with dual vCPUs and four gigabytes of memory for basic development and lightweight utility workloads.
